context.project_description: A system to track and report token usage for each LLM query across all supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) by instrumenting provider calls with a benchmark hook that captures token counts from responses.
key_requirements: Instrument existing LLM providers to count tokens consumed per operation
Add a simple way to access token usage data via SDK or API endpoint
Support basic logging of token counts for debugging/monitoring purposes
constraints: Must not significantly impact performance - the token counting should add minimal overhead to existing flows
Need support for all current LLM providers - OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama
Integrates with existing architecture patterns (instrumentation system, logging)
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py provides the existing instrumentation framework that can be used to wrap LLM calls via BenchmarkHook subclasses; fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines protocols that all provider implementations must conform to; fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py contain the actual provider implementations
stakeholders: Developers who want to know what their queries cost and debug performance issues
Platform Operators who need monitoring and alerting capabilities around LLM costs
Product Managers interested in understanding usage patterns for capacity planning
System Administrators who want observability into resource consumption

in_scope: Instrument existing LLM providers to count tokens consumed per operation
Add a simple way to access token usage data via SDK or API endpoint
Support basic logging of token counts for debugging/monitoring purposes
out_of_scope: Complex billing system integration (actual cost calculations)
UI dashboard for viewing token usage trends over time

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Provides the existing instrumentation framework that can be used to wrap LLM calls
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Foundation: All provider implementations must conform to these protocols
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — Implementation file for OpenAI provider with chat, embed, and vision methods
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Implementation file for Anthropic provider with chat and vision methods
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Implementation file for Cohere provider with chat, embed, and rerank methods
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Implementation file for Ollama provider with chat, embed, rerank, and vision methods
fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py — Entry point for users of the SDK that will need token usage data access
fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py — HTTP API entrypoints that may include token metrics in responses
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_counter.py — Implementation of a BenchmarkHook subclass (TokenCountingHook) for counting tokens
fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/__init__.py — Exports token counting functionality and utilities, sets up hook configuration
fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py — Data models for representing usage metrics (total tokens, etc.)

assumptions.assumption: Token counting happens at the provider level (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) - they provide APIs for this or we need to count manually
assumptions.impact: If providers don't expose token counts in their APIs, we'll need to implement manual counting logic which adds complexity and potential inaccuracies. If we assume they do provide it but they don't, the tracking will miss tokens entirely.

assumptions.assumption: Tracking will be enabled/disabled via configuration
assumptions.impact: If this mechanism is not properly implemented or documented, users won't be able to control token tracking which could lead to performance issues in production environments where it's unexpectedly turned on.

assumptions.assumption: This is primarily about consumption tracking rather than cost calculation (though related)
assumptions.impact: If cost calculation becomes necessary, we would need to add currency conversion logic and pricing models which wasn't part of the scope. This assumption could also impact whether we track prompt/completion separately vs total tokens.

assumptions.assumption: The system already has instrumentation hooks that can be leveraged
assumptions.impact: If this is incorrect, we'd have to build a custom solution instead of leveraging existing patterns, leading to more complex code and potential compatibility issues with other parts of the system.

assumptions.assumption: Token definition: Total tokens consumed for each call
assumptions.impact: If we need different granularity (prompt vs completion), this assumption would require changing data models, API responses, and potentially UI/CLI interfaces that expect total token counts.
If long-term storage is required later, we'd need to refactor the architecture to use persistent databases instead of just memory-based structures. This also affects how we expose data via APIs.

assumptions.assumption: All providers have token counting capability built-in or can be implemented manually
assumptions.impact: If any provider doesn't support token counts at all, we'd need to implement custom logic for that specific provider type which could introduce edge case bugs and inconsistencies.

assumptions.assumption: Logging with structured data enough, no need for explicit APIs yet
assumptions.impact: If users later require programmatic access (e.g., monitoring tools), we'll need to add API endpoints or additional SDK methods that weren't initially planned.

approaches.name: Instrumentation Hook with Thread-Local Storage
approaches.description: Uses BenchmarkHook protocol to intercept LLM calls and extract token usage from provider responses using thread-local storage for concurrent safety.
pros: Thread-safe storage avoiding global state race conditions
Minimal impact on existing provider interfaces
Supports all major LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Follows existing instrumentation patterns in codebase
cons: Current LLM APIs return simple types (str/list) rather than objects with usage fields making extraction difficult
Requires hook registration at engine initialization to work properly across SDK/API layers
No automatic integration with API responses without schema updates
SDK method get_last_token_usage() not implemented in existing codebase
best_for: Systems requiring detailed LLM cost tracking and observability
Multi-provider environments where token usage needs to be aggregated across different services

approaches.name: Raw Response Interception with Provider-Specific Parsing
approaches.description: Modifies provider call flow to capture raw HTTP responses before type conversion, parsing tokens from structured response bodies.
pros: Guaranteed access to token information since it's captured at wire level
More reliable than trying to extract from converted return types
Works with all existing LLM provider contracts without modification
Can handle cases where providers don't expose tokens in object format
cons: Requires deeper integration changes across multiple layers
May break some existing abstraction boundaries in the SDK/API layer
Complexity increases due to need for HTTP-level interception and parsing
Potential performance overhead from additional serialization/deserialization steps
best_for: Production systems where token accuracy is critical
Environments with custom or proprietary LLM providers that don't expose usage metadata

architecture.reasoning: This approach is recommended because it follows existing architectural patterns (BenchmarkHook protocol, InstrumentedProxy wrappers) and builds upon the established instrumentation system rather than overhauling core provider contracts. The thread-local storage improves on previous global registry approaches by preventing race conditions in concurrent environments while maintaining simplicity for single-request contexts. However, this approach faces two major issues that must be addressed: 1) Current LLM providers return simple types (strings/lists) instead of structured objects containing token usage metadata, making extraction impossible without modifying the provider interfaces or interception layer; 2) The current SDK and API layers don't register hooks properly for instrumentation to capture calls from those entry points. While this approach aligns with existing patterns and is more maintainable than raw HTTP interception, it's only feasible if we address these architectural limitations in the LLM provider contracts.

key_tradeoffs.thread_safety_vs_global_state: Using thread-local storage instead of global registry prevents race conditions in concurrent environments while maintaining simplicity for single-request contexts
key_tradeoffs.provider_compatibility_vs_implementation: Supporting multiple LLM provider response formats requires complex conditional logic that could be simplified with more standardized interfaces or duck typing approaches
key_tradeoffs.automatic_tracking_vs_explicit_control: Module-level hook registration provides automatic instrumentation but lacks runtime configurability; explicit registration offers better control over when hooks are active

technology_considerations: threading.local() for thread-safe token usage storage to avoid global state issues
dataclasses with frozen=True for immutable token usage data structures
UUID generation for unique operation IDs in concurrent environments
Protocol-based BenchmarkHook interface for extensible instrumentation system
duck typing and hasattr checks for provider-agnostic response parsing
architecture.scope_statement: Implement token usage tracking by adding BenchmarkHook-based instrumentation that captures LLM call metadata via thread-local storage, requires updating LLM provider interfaces to support raw response access for accurate token parsing

adrs.title: ADR: Thread-Local Storage for Token Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The need to track detailed token usage statistics (input + output tokens) across different LLM providers in a concurrent environment while avoiding global state issues that could cause race conditions.
adrs.decision: Use thread-local storage instead of a global registry for storing and retrieving token usage data, ensuring each request's token tracking is isolated and thread-safe. This approach replaces the previous global dictionary to prevent concurrency issues but maintains access patterns through uuid-based operation IDs.
adrs.rationale: Thread-local storage provides isolation per-request context without requiring explicit locking mechanisms or global state management. It works well with concurrent API handling since each request has its own copy of the registry. The solution uses UUIDs as operation IDs for tracking, which aligns with how instrumentation systems generate unique identifiers in existing codebase patterns.
consequences: Each HTTP request will have isolated token usage data that doesn't interfere with other requests
Thread safety is guaranteed by using threading.local() instead of shared global variables
The implementation requires updating all entry points (SDK and API) to properly track operation IDs through context managers or explicit propagation
API responses must be extended to include token_usage fields in QueryResponse/ChatResponse schemas
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Global dictionary registry – rejected because it introduces race conditions under concurrent access, as demonstrated by data flow maps showing API endpoints running concurrently and SDK methods being called from different threads

Alternative: Context manager with explicit operation ID passing through call stack – rejected because requires significant refactoring of existing code paths in fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py and fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py to pass around the UUIDs, which would be error-prone and invasive

adrs.title: ADR: Instrumentation Hook Registration for LLM Operations
adrs.context: The requirement to automatically capture token usage data from all LLM provider calls (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) without manually wrapping each call site.
adrs.decision: Implement a BenchmarkHook-based system using the existing instrumentation framework in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py. Register hooks explicitly during engine initialization rather than relying on module-level instances that may not be active in all code paths (SDK and API layers).
adrs.rationale: This approach leverages the established pattern already present in the codebase for benchmarking and monitoring, allowing hook registration to be controlled programmatically per execution context instead of assuming hooks are always registered at import time. It also prevents issues with missing registrations during SDK/API calls where module-level instances wouldn't be active.
consequences: Engine initialization must explicitly register token counting hooks to ensure they fire for LLM operations
SDK and API entry points will need to trigger the same instrumentation setup or access pre-registered hooks
Hook registration becomes more configurable but also requires explicit setup in new code paths (e.g., during engine creation)
The TokenCountingHook needs to be registered with fitz_ai.core.instrumentation.register_hook() before LLM calls occur
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Module-level hook instance – rejected because it assumes hooks are available at import time and doesn't integrate properly with SDK/API layers where explicit registration is needed for control over when hooks activate

Alternative: Direct proxy wrapping of all provider methods – rejected due to invasive changes required to modify existing provider interfaces which already define return types as simple strings or lists

adrs.title: ADR: Provider-Specific Token Usage Extraction Logic
adrs.context: Different LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) return varying response structures that need to be parsed for token information.
adrs.decision: Implement provider-specific extraction logic in the _extract_token_usage method of TokenCountingHook with defensive coding practices to handle various response formats gracefully while acknowledging current limitations in API contract types (strings instead of full objects).
adrs.rationale: The implementation handles different LLM providers by checking for standard attributes and falling back gracefully. However, this approach recognizes that existing provider interfaces return simple types like str or list[float], which don't contain usage metadata at the point where token counting hooks are invoked. This limitation must be addressed via either modifying API contracts to capture raw responses before type conversion or ensuring hooks can intercept at a lower level.
consequences: Token extraction will currently fail for most real-world LLM calls because they return simple types (str, list[float]) instead of complex objects with .usage attributes
The current design cannot extract token usage from any actual provider call without modifying the provider interface contracts or adding middleware to capture raw responses before processing
All extraction logic within _extract_token_usage is defensive and will silently return None if no appropriate fields are found, preventing crashes but resulting in missing data
Future enhancements could modify LLM client layer to preserve response objects until post-processing for token counting
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Modify provider interfaces to return complex objects with usage metadata – rejected because it would break existing APIs and requires significant refactoring of all 4+ provider implementations

Alternative: Instrument at lower level before type conversion in providers – rejected because this is outside current scope and affects core LLM infrastructure that may not be instrumentable at this layer

adrs.title: ADR: SDK Integration for Token Usage Access
adrs.context: The need to expose token usage data directly from the fitz SDK layer (fitz.get_last_token_usage()) so users can access cost information easily after making queries.
adrs.decision: Implement get_last_token_usage() in fitz class by tracking the last operation ID through thread-local storage and accessing it via existing registry mechanism. This requires extending the thread-safe token usage system to include a way of tracking which was the most recently executed operation.
adrs.rationale: Since SDK methods are implemented independently from API routes, we need an explicit approach for storing the most recent operation ID in the current execution context (using thread-local storage). The pattern should match what's already established in instrumentation systems and be consistent with how other contextual data is managed throughout the system. This allows SDK users to get access to token usage without needing to interact with low-level hooks directly.
consequences: SDK user can call fitz.get_last_token_usage() after any LLM operation to retrieve cost info for that specific query
Thread-local storage must be enhanced to track last used operation ID alongside the registry of all operations during a request
The method will only work if token counting hooks are properly registered and active in SDK execution context
Requires updating documentation to explain how to access token usage data through SDK interface
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Provide no direct SDK access, requiring manual hook inspection – rejected because it makes the feature unusable for typical SDK users who expect simple API access rather than low-level integration

Alternative: Create a context manager that automatically tracks last operation ID – rejected because requires adding new middleware patterns that would complicate existing execution flows unnecessarily

components.name: TokenUsage
components.purpose: Represents detailed token usage statistics for LLM operations

components.name: TokenUsageContext
components.purpose: Contextual information about where token usage occurred

components.name: TokenCountingHook
components.purpose: A benchmark hook that counts tokens consumed by LLM operations
responsibilities: Generate unique operation ID for tracking during call start
Extract token usage from different provider response formats during call end
Store extracted token usage in thread-local storage
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

integration_points: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — TokenCountingHook registers with BenchmarkHook protocol and integrates via register_hook() function
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — All LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, VisionProvider) are instrumented through InstrumentedProxy wrapping
fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py — SDK entry points (ask/query) need to integrate with token usage tracking via thread-local context management
fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py — API endpoints (query/chat) must propagate operation IDs and include token_usage in response schemas

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenUsage:
    """Represents detailed token usage statistics for LLM operations."""
    
    # Total tokens consumed (input + output)
    total_tokens: int
    
    # Input tokens specifically
    prompt_tokens: int = 0
    
    # Output tokens specifically  
    completion_tokens: int = 0
    
    # For embedding operations, may be None or zero depending on provider
    embedding_tokens: Optional[int] = None

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenUsageContext:
    """Contextual information about where token usage occurred."""
    
    layer: str  # e.g., "llm"
    plugin_name: str  # e.g., "openai", "anthropic"
    method: str  # e.g., "chat", "embed", "describe_image"
    
    # Additional metadata for debugging
    operation_id: Optional[str] = None  # UUID or other identifier
    
    # Per-method details (if available)
    request_args: Dict[str, Any] = None  
    response_data: Dict[str, Any] = None

# Thread-local storage to avoid global state issues
import threading
_token_usage_storage = threading.local()  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures

def get_token_usage(operation_id: str) -> Optional[TokenUsage]:
    """Get token usage for a specific operation."""
    if not hasattr(_token_usage_storage, 'registry'):
        return None
    return _token_usage_storage.registry.get(operation_id)

def set_token_usage(operation_id: str, usage: TokenUsage) -> None:
    """Store token usage for a specific operation."""  
    if not hasattr(_token_usage_storage, 'registry'):
        _token_usage_storage.registry = {}
    _token_usage_storage.registry[operation_id] = usage

def clear_token_usage() -> None:
    """Clear all stored token usage data."""
    if hasattr(_token_usage_storage, 'registry'):
        _token_usage_storage.registry.clear()
artifacts.purpose: Provides data models and thread-local storage for tracking LLM token usage statistics across different providers.

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_counter.py
import uuid
from typing import Any, Optional
from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import BenchmarkHook
from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import (
    TokenUsage,
    set_token_usage
)

class TokenCountingHook(BenchmarkHook):
    """
    A benchmark hook that counts tokens consumed by LLM operations.
    
    This hook captures token usage from provider responses and stores it 
    for later access via the global registry. It supports all major LLM
    providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) with appropriate parsing
    logic per provider type.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._operation_id: Optional[str] = None

    def on_call_start(
        self,
        layer: str,
        plugin_name: str,
        method: str,
        args: tuple,
        kwargs: dict,
    ) -> Any:
        # Generate a unique operation ID for tracking
        self._operation_id = str(uuid.uuid4())  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
        return self._operation_id

    def on_call_end(
        self,
        context: Any,
        result: Any,
        error: Exception | None,
    ) -> None:
        if error is not None or self._operation_id is None:
            # Skip token counting for failed calls
            return
            
        try:
            operation_id = context  # This should be the UUID from on_call_start
            
            # Extract token usage based on response type and provider
            token_usage = self._extract_token_usage(result, operation_id)
            
            if token_usage is not None:
                set_token_usage(operation_id, token_usage)
                
        except Exception as e:
            # Log but don't fail the main execution path
            import logging
            logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
            logger.warning(f"Failed to extract token usage: {e}")

    def _extract_token_usage(self, result: Any, operation_id: str) -> Optional[TokenUsage]:
        """
        Extract token usage from different provider response formats.
        
        Returns:
            TokenUsage object if tokens were successfully parsed, None otherwise
        """
        # Handle different LLM providers' response structures
        
        # OpenAI-style responses (including chat and embedding)
        if hasattr(result, 'usage') and hasattr(result.usage, 'total_tokens'):
            try:
                total = result.usage.total_tokens
                prompt = getattr(result.usage, 'prompt_tokens', 0) 
                completion = getattr(result.usage, 'completion_tokens', 0)
                
                return TokenUsage(
                    total_tokens=total,
                    prompt_tokens=prompt,
                    completion_tokens=completion
                )
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Anthropic-style responses (may contain metadata or usage fields in response objects)
        if hasattr(result, '__dict__') and 'usage' in result.__dict__:
            try:
                usage = result.usage
                total = getattr(usage, 'total_tokens', 0) 
                prompt = getattr(usage, 'input_tokens', 0)
                completion = getattr(usage, 'output_tokens', 0)
                
                return TokenUsage(
                    total_tokens=total,
                    prompt_tokens=prompt,
                    completion_tokens=completion
                )
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Cohere-style responses (may have meta.billed_units fields)  
        if hasattr(result, 'meta') and hasattr(result.meta, 'billed_units'):
            try:
                billed = result.meta.billed_units
                input_tokens = getattr(billed, 'input_tokens', 0)
                output_tokens = getattr(billed, 'output_tokens', 0)
                total_tokens = input_tokens + output_tokens
                
                return TokenUsage(
                    total_tokens=total_tokens,
                    prompt_tokens=input_tokens,
                    completion_tokens=output_tokens
                )
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Ollama-style responses (no built-in token tracking - estimate as fallback) 
        if hasattr(result, 'response') and isinstance(result.response, str):
            try:
                # For now we don't extract tokens from Ollama since it doesn't provide them
                # We could potentially estimate based on string length or other heuristics,
                # but for simplicity we'll skip this until explicitly needed
                return None
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Handle raw string responses (e.g., vision provider outputs)
        if isinstance(result, str):
            # String responses from chat/vision providers don't typically include token info.
            # We can still store a placeholder or ignore them depending on requirements. 
            return None
            
        # For embedding operations that might be handled specially
        # Cohere embeddings have different response structure than other models  
        if hasattr(result, 'embeddings'):
            try:
                # If this is an embedding result with token usage info in metadata
                if hasattr(result, 'meta') and hasattr(result.meta, 'billed_units'):
                    billed = result.meta.billed_units
                    input_tokens = getattr(billed, 'input_tokens', 0)
                    
                    return TokenUsage(
                        total_tokens=input_tokens,
                        prompt_tokens=input_tokens,
                        completion_tokens=0,
                        embedding_tokens=input_tokens
                    )
                else:
                    # No token info available for embeddings in this case
                    return None
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Default: no tokens found or parsing error
        return None

# Module-level instance of the hook (for easy registration)
token_counting_hook = TokenCountingHook()
artifacts.purpose: Implements a benchmark hook that intercepts LLM calls to extract and store token usage statistics from provider responses.

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/__init__.py
"""Instrumentation utilities for LLM operations including token counting."""

from .token_counter import TokenCountingHook, token_counting_hook

__all__ = [
    "TokenCountingHook",
    "token_counting_hook"
]
artifacts.purpose: Exports the token counting instrumentation components for easy integration into LLM provider systems.

approaches.name: Instrumentation Hook with Thread-Local Storage
approaches.description: Uses BenchmarkHook protocol to intercept LLM calls and extract token usage from provider responses using thread-local storage for concurrent safety.
pros: Thread-safe storage avoiding global state race conditions
Minimal impact on existing provider interfaces
Supports all major LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Follows existing instrumentation patterns in codebase
cons: Current LLM APIs return simple types (str/list) rather than objects with usage fields making extraction difficult
Requires hook registration at engine initialization to work properly across SDK/API layers
No automatic integration with API responses without schema updates
SDK method get_last_token_usage() not implemented in existing codebase
best_for: Systems requiring detailed LLM cost tracking and observability
Multi-provider environments where token usage needs to be aggregated across different services

approaches.name: Raw Response Interception with Provider-Specific Parsing
approaches.description: Modifies provider call flow to capture raw HTTP responses before type conversion, parsing tokens from structured response bodies.
pros: Guaranteed access to token information since it's captured at wire level
More reliable than trying to extract from converted return types
Works with all existing LLM provider contracts without modification
Can handle cases where providers don't expose tokens in object format
cons: Requires deeper integration changes across multiple layers
May break some existing abstraction boundaries in the SDK/API layer
Complexity increases due to need for HTTP-level interception and parsing
Potential performance overhead from additional serialization/deserialization steps
best_for: Production systems where token accuracy is critical
Environments with custom or proprietary LLM providers that don't expose usage metadata

architecture.reasoning: This approach is recommended because it follows existing architectural patterns (BenchmarkHook protocol, InstrumentedProxy wrappers) and builds upon the established instrumentation system rather than overhauling core provider contracts. The thread-local storage improves on previous global registry approaches by preventing race conditions in concurrent environments while maintaining simplicity for single-request contexts. However, this approach faces two major issues that must be addressed: 1) Current LLM providers return simple types (strings/lists) instead of structured objects containing token usage metadata, making extraction impossible without modifying the provider interfaces or interception layer; 2) The current SDK and API layers don't register hooks properly for instrumentation to capture calls from those entry points. While this approach aligns with existing patterns and is more maintainable than raw HTTP interception, it's only feasible if we address these architectural limitations in the LLM provider contracts.

key_tradeoffs.thread_safety_vs_global_state: Using thread-local storage instead of global registry prevents race conditions in concurrent environments while maintaining simplicity for single-request contexts
key_tradeoffs.provider_compatibility_vs_implementation: Supporting multiple LLM provider response formats requires complex conditional logic that could be simplified with more standardized interfaces or duck typing approaches
key_tradeoffs.automatic_tracking_vs_explicit_control: Module-level hook registration provides automatic instrumentation but lacks runtime configurability; explicit registration offers better control over when hooks are active

technology_considerations: threading.local() for thread-safe token usage storage to avoid global state issues
dataclasses with frozen=True for immutable token usage data structures
UUID generation for unique operation IDs in concurrent environments
Protocol-based BenchmarkHook interface for extensible instrumentation system
duck typing and hasattr checks for provider-agnostic response parsing
architecture.scope_statement: Implement token usage tracking by adding BenchmarkHook-based instrumentation that captures LLM call metadata via thread-local storage, requires updating LLM provider interfaces to support raw response access for accurate token parsing

adrs.title: ADR: Thread-Local Storage for Token Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The need to track detailed token usage statistics (input + output tokens) across different LLM providers in a concurrent environment while avoiding global state issues that could cause race conditions.
adrs.decision: Use thread-local storage instead of a global registry for storing and retrieving token usage data, ensuring each request's token tracking is isolated and thread-safe. This approach replaces the previous global dictionary to prevent concurrency issues but maintains access patterns through uuid-based operation IDs.
adrs.rationale: Thread-local storage provides isolation per-request context without requiring explicit locking mechanisms or global state management. It works well with concurrent API handling since each request has its own copy of the registry. The solution uses UUIDs as operation IDs for tracking, which aligns with how instrumentation systems generate unique identifiers in existing codebase patterns.
consequences: Each HTTP request will have isolated token usage data that doesn't interfere with other requests
Thread safety is guaranteed by using threading.local() instead of shared global variables
The implementation requires updating all entry points (SDK and API) to properly track operation IDs through context managers or explicit propagation
API responses must be extended to include token_usage fields in QueryResponse/ChatResponse schemas
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Global dictionary registry – rejected because it introduces race conditions under concurrent access, as demonstrated by data flow maps showing API endpoints running concurrently and SDK methods being called from different threads

Alternative: Context manager with explicit operation ID passing through call stack – rejected because requires significant refactoring of existing code paths in fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py and fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py to pass around the UUIDs, which would be error-prone and invasive

adrs.title: ADR: Instrumentation Hook Registration for LLM Operations
adrs.context: The requirement to automatically capture token usage data from all LLM provider calls (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) without manually wrapping each call site.
adrs.decision: Implement a BenchmarkHook-based system using the existing instrumentation framework in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py. Register hooks explicitly during engine initialization rather than relying on module-level instances that may not be active in all code paths (SDK and API layers).
adrs.rationale: This approach leverages the established pattern already present in the codebase for benchmarking and monitoring, allowing hook registration to be controlled programmatically per execution context instead of assuming hooks are always registered at import time. It also prevents issues with missing registrations during SDK/API calls where module-level instances wouldn't be active.
consequences: Engine initialization must explicitly register token counting hooks to ensure they fire for LLM operations
SDK and API entry points will need to trigger the same instrumentation setup or access pre-registered hooks
Hook registration becomes more configurable but also requires explicit setup in new code paths (e.g., during engine creation)
The TokenCountingHook needs to be registered with fitz_ai.core.instrumentation.register_hook() before LLM calls occur
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Module-level hook instance – rejected because it assumes hooks are available at import time and doesn't integrate properly with SDK/API layers where explicit registration is needed for control over when hooks activate

Alternative: Direct proxy wrapping of all provider methods – rejected due to invasive changes required to modify existing provider interfaces which already define return types as simple strings or lists

adrs.title: ADR: Provider-Specific Token Usage Extraction Logic
adrs.context: Different LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) return varying response structures that need to be parsed for token information.
adrs.decision: Implement provider-specific extraction logic in the _extract_token_usage method of TokenCountingHook with defensive coding practices to handle various response formats gracefully while acknowledging current limitations in API contract types (strings instead of full objects).
adrs.rationale: The implementation handles different LLM providers by checking for standard attributes and falling back gracefully. However, this approach recognizes that existing provider interfaces return simple types like str or list[float], which don't contain usage metadata at the point where token counting hooks are invoked. This limitation must be addressed via either modifying API contracts to capture raw responses before type conversion or ensuring hooks can intercept at a lower level.
consequences: Token extraction will currently fail for most real-world LLM calls because they return simple types (str, list[float]) instead of complex objects with .usage attributes
The current design cannot extract token usage from any actual provider call without modifying the provider interface contracts or adding middleware to capture raw responses before processing
All extraction logic within _extract_token_usage is defensive and will silently return None if no appropriate fields are found, preventing crashes but resulting in missing data
Future enhancements could modify LLM client layer to preserve response objects until post-processing for token counting
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Modify provider interfaces to return complex objects with usage metadata – rejected because it would break existing APIs and requires significant refactoring of all 4+ provider implementations

Alternative: Instrument at lower level before type conversion in providers – rejected because this is outside current scope and affects core LLM infrastructure that may not be instrumentable at this layer

adrs.title: ADR: SDK Integration for Token Usage Access
adrs.context: The need to expose token usage data directly from the fitz SDK layer (fitz.get_last_token_usage()) so users can access cost information easily after making queries.
adrs.decision: Implement get_last_token_usage() in fitz class by tracking the last operation ID through thread-local storage and accessing it via existing registry mechanism. This requires extending the thread-safe token usage system to include a way of tracking which was the most recently executed operation.
adrs.rationale: Since SDK methods are implemented independently from API routes, we need an explicit approach for storing the most recent operation ID in the current execution context (using thread-local storage). The pattern should match what's already established in instrumentation systems and be consistent with how other contextual data is managed throughout the system. This allows SDK users to get access to token usage without needing to interact with low-level hooks directly.
consequences: SDK user can call fitz.get_last_token_usage() after any LLM operation to retrieve cost info for that specific query
Thread-local storage must be enhanced to track last used operation ID alongside the registry of all operations during a request
The method will only work if token counting hooks are properly registered and active in SDK execution context
Requires updating documentation to explain how to access token usage data through SDK interface
alternatives_considered: Alternative: Provide no direct SDK access, requiring manual hook inspection – rejected because it makes the feature unusable for typical SDK users who expect simple API access rather than low-level integration

Alternative: Create a context manager that automatically tracks last operation ID – rejected because requires adding new middleware patterns that would complicate existing execution flows unnecessarily

components.name: TokenUsage
components.purpose: Represents detailed token usage statistics for LLM operations

components.name: TokenUsageContext
components.purpose: Contextual information about where token usage occurred

components.name: TokenCountingHook
components.purpose: A benchmark hook that counts tokens consumed by LLM operations
responsibilities: Generate unique operation ID for tracking during call start
Extract token usage from different provider response formats during call end
Store extracted token usage in thread-local storage
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

integration_points: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — TokenCountingHook registers with BenchmarkHook protocol and integrates via register_hook() function
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — All LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, VisionProvider) are instrumented through InstrumentedProxy wrapping
fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py — SDK entry points (ask/query) need to integrate with token usage tracking via thread-local context management
fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py — API endpoints (query/chat) must propagate operation IDs and include token_usage in response schemas

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenUsage:
    """Represents detailed token usage statistics for LLM operations."""
    
    # Total tokens consumed (input + output)
    total_tokens: int
    
    # Input tokens specifically
    prompt_tokens: int = 0
    
    # Output tokens specifically  
    completion_tokens: int = 0
    
    # For embedding operations, may be None or zero depending on provider
    embedding_tokens: Optional[int] = None

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenUsageContext:
    """Contextual information about where token usage occurred."""
    
    layer: str  # e.g., "llm"
    plugin_name: str  # e.g., "openai", "anthropic"
    method: str  # e.g., "chat", "embed", "describe_image"
    
    # Additional metadata for debugging
    operation_id: Optional[str] = None  # UUID or other identifier
    
    # Per-method details (if available)
    request_args: Dict[str, Any] = None  
    response_data: Dict[str, Any] = None

# Thread-local storage to avoid global state issues
import threading
_token_usage_storage = threading.local()  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures

def get_token_usage(operation_id: str) -> Optional[TokenUsage]:
    """Get token usage for a specific operation."""
    if not hasattr(_token_usage_storage, 'registry'):
        return None
    return _token_usage_storage.registry.get(operation_id)

def set_token_usage(operation_id: str, usage: TokenUsage) -> None:
    """Store token usage for a specific operation."""  
    if not hasattr(_token_usage_storage, 'registry'):
        _token_usage_storage.registry = {}
    _token_usage_storage.registry[operation_id] = usage

def clear_token_usage() -> None:
    """Clear all stored token usage data."""
    if hasattr(_token_usage_storage, 'registry'):
        _token_usage_storage.registry.clear()
artifacts.purpose: Provides data models and thread-local storage for tracking LLM token usage statistics across different providers.

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_counter.py
import uuid
from typing import Any, Optional
from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import BenchmarkHook
from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import (
    TokenUsage,
    set_token_usage
)

class TokenCountingHook(BenchmarkHook):
    """
    A benchmark hook that counts tokens consumed by LLM operations.
    
    This hook captures token usage from provider responses and stores it 
    for later access via the global registry. It supports all major LLM
    providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) with appropriate parsing
    logic per provider type.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self._operation_id: Optional[str] = None

    def on_call_start(
        self,
        layer: str,
        plugin_name: str,
        method: str,
        args: tuple,
        kwargs: dict,
    ) -> Any:
        # Generate a unique operation ID for tracking
        self._operation_id = str(uuid.uuid4())  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
        return self._operation_id

    def on_call_end(
        self,
        context: Any,
        result: Any,
        error: Exception | None,
    ) -> None:
        if error is not None or self._operation_id is None:
            # Skip token counting for failed calls
            return
            
        try:
            operation_id = context  # This should be the UUID from on_call_start
            
            # Extract token usage based on response type and provider
            token_usage = self._extract_token_usage(result, operation_id)
            
            if token_usage is not None:
                set_token_usage(operation_id, token_usage)
                
        except Exception as e:
            # Log but don't fail the main execution path
            import logging
            logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)  # [VERIFY] method not found in signatures
            logger.warning(f"Failed to extract token usage: {e}")

    def _extract_token_usage(self, result: Any, operation_id: str) -> Optional[TokenUsage]:
        """
        Extract token usage from different provider response formats.
        
        Returns:
            TokenUsage object if tokens were successfully parsed, None otherwise
        """
        # Handle different LLM providers' response structures
        
        # OpenAI-style responses (including chat and embedding)
        if hasattr(result, 'usage') and hasattr(result.usage, 'total_tokens'):
            try:
                total = result.usage.total_tokens
                prompt = getattr(result.usage, 'prompt_tokens', 0) 
                completion = getattr(result.usage, 'completion_tokens', 0)
                
                return TokenUsage(
                    total_tokens=total,
                    prompt_tokens=prompt,
                    completion_tokens=completion
                )
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Anthropic-style responses (may contain metadata or usage fields in response objects)
        if hasattr(result, '__dict__') and 'usage' in result.__dict__:
            try:
                usage = result.usage
                total = getattr(usage, 'total_tokens', 0) 
                prompt = getattr(usage, 'input_tokens', 0)
                completion = getattr(usage, 'output_tokens', 0)
                
                return TokenUsage(
                    total_tokens=total,
                    prompt_tokens=prompt,
                    completion_tokens=completion
                )
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Cohere-style responses (may have meta.billed_units fields)  
        if hasattr(result, 'meta') and hasattr(result.meta, 'billed_units'):
            try:
                billed = result.meta.billed_units
                input_tokens = getattr(billed, 'input_tokens', 0)
                output_tokens = getattr(billed, 'output_tokens', 0)
                total_tokens = input_tokens + output_tokens
                
                return TokenUsage(
                    total_tokens=total_tokens,
                    prompt_tokens=input_tokens,
                    completion_tokens=output_tokens
                )
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Ollama-style responses (no built-in token tracking - estimate as fallback) 
        if hasattr(result, 'response') and isinstance(result.response, str):
            try:
                # For now we don't extract tokens from Ollama since it doesn't provide them
                # We could potentially estimate based on string length or other heuristics,
                # but for simplicity we'll skip this until explicitly needed
                return None
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Handle raw string responses (e.g., vision provider outputs)
        if isinstance(result, str):
            # String responses from chat/vision providers don't typically include token info.
            # We can still store a placeholder or ignore them depending on requirements. 
            return None
            
        # For embedding operations that might be handled specially
        # Cohere embeddings have different response structure than other models  
        if hasattr(result, 'embeddings'):
            try:
                # If this is an embedding result with token usage info in metadata
                if hasattr(result, 'meta') and hasattr(result.meta, 'billed_units'):
                    billed = result.meta.billed_units
                    input_tokens = getattr(billed, 'input_tokens', 0)
                    
                    return TokenUsage(
                        total_tokens=input_tokens,
                        prompt_tokens=input_tokens,
                        completion_tokens=0,
                        embedding_tokens=input_tokens
                    )
                else:
                    # No token info available for embeddings in this case
                    return None
            except Exception:
                pass
                
        # Default: no tokens found or parsing error
        return None

# Module-level instance of the hook (for easy registration)
token_counting_hook = TokenCountingHook()
artifacts.purpose: Implements a benchmark hook that intercepts LLM calls to extract and store token usage statistics from provider responses.

artifacts.content:
# fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/__init__.py
"""Instrumentation utilities for LLM operations including token counting."""

from .token_counter import TokenCountingHook, token_counting_hook

__all__ = [
    "TokenCountingHook",
    "token_counting_hook"
]
artifacts.purpose: Exports the token counting instrumentation components for easy integration into LLM provider systems.

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
deliverables: Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Analyze Existing LLM Provider Interfaces
phases.objective: Identify current LLM provider protocols and their lack of token tracking capabilities
deliverables: Documentation of existing ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, RerankProvider, VisionProvider interfaces
Analysis of how providers implement these interfaces without token counting
key_risks: Assuming all providers follow same pattern when they may vary in implementation details

phases.name: Design Token Tracking Protocol Extension
phases.objective: Define how token usage should be tracked and reported across all LLM providers
deliverables: Updated provider protocol definitions with optional token tracking fields
TokenUsage data structure specification
key_risks: Breaking changes to existing interfaces if not designed carefully

phases.name: Implement Token Counting in OpenAI Providers
phases.objective: Add token usage tracking to OpenAI chat, embedding and vision providers
deliverables: Modified OpenAIChat class with input/output token counting
Updated OpenAIEmbedding class with token tracking capability
Enhanced OpenAIVision class to track image processing tokens
key_risks: OpenAI SDK behavior may differ between versions in how it exposes usage metrics

phases.name: Implement Token Counting in Anthropic Providers
phases.objective: Add token usage tracking to Anthropic chat and vision providers
deliverables: Modified AnthropicChat class with input/output token counting
Enhanced AnthropicVision class to track image processing tokens
key_risks: Anthropic SDK may not expose usage data in same way as OpenAI

phases.name: Implement Token Counting in Cohere Providers
phases.objective: Add token usage tracking to Cohere chat, embedding and rerank providers
deliverables: Modified CohereChat class with input/output token counting
Updated CohereEmbedding class with token tracking capability
Enhanced CohereRerank class to track query/document processing tokens
key_risks: Cohere SDK behavior may not expose usage metrics directly in same way as OpenAI

phases.name: Implement Token Counting in Ollama Providers
phases.objective: Add token usage tracking to Ollama chat, embedding and rerank providers
deliverables: Modified OllamaChat class with input/output token counting
Updated OllamaEmbedding class with token tracking capability
Enhanced OllamaRerank class to track query/document processing tokens
Improved OllamaVision class for image processing token tracking
key_risks: Ollama may not provide explicit token usage in responses or require manual calculation

phases.name: Add Centralized Logging/Metrics Layer
phases.objective: Create unified system to aggregate and report token usage per query across all providers
deliverables: New instrumentation hooks for tracking token counts
TokenUsageReporter class that aggregates metrics from different providers
Integration with existing logging infrastructure
key_risks: Performance impact of additional instrumentation on request latency

risks.description: LLM provider implementations lack built-in token counting mechanisms
risks.mitigation: Modify base provider protocols to include token tracking and update all concrete implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) to capture input/output token usage during calls
risks.contingency: Implement a fallback mechanism that estimates tokens based on character count or uses external libraries for tokenization
risks.verification: Verify that each LLM provider returns token counts in their responses or through new methods

risks.description: Missing instrumentation layer for centralized token tracking and logging
risks.mitigation: Extend the existing instrumentation system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py to include token usage hooks that can be registered by LLM providers
risks.contingency: Add a simple middleware or decorator pattern that wraps LLM calls to capture and log token counts per request
risks.verification: Confirm that token usage is consistently logged for all LLM provider interactions

risks.description: Implementation of token tracking requires significant engineering effort across multiple modules
risks.mitigation: Break down implementation into incremental changes and test each LLM provider separately before integration
risks.contingency: Prioritize core providers (OpenAI, Anthropic) first to get basic functionality working, then extend to others
risks.verification: Ensure that token tracking is correctly implemented for at least two major LLM providers before deployment

recommended_contingencies: Implement a fallback mechanism using character-based estimation for token counting if precise methods fail
Prioritize core LLM provider implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic) for initial token tracking capability
Use existing middleware or decorator patterns to wrap LLM calls without extensive architectural changes

Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
